skill_purpose: Review absolute, omnibus, and unsubstantiated superlative efficacy claims for qualification and substantiation requirements.
summary: Detects superlative, absolute, and testimonial efficacy claims; guides reviewers to verify substantiation exists before publication and to qualify omnibus wording.
review_guidance: TRIGGER: Absolute efficacy, cure-style, or omnibus superiority language in headlines or body copy. ACTION: Identify whether substantiation is referenced or available on file. CHECK: Testimonials, before/after claims, and unqualified superlatives. ESCALATE IF: Claim is absolute with no substantiation trace — route to Rule Corpus for binding decision.

detection_patterns.description: Efficacy or testimonial claims that require substantiation or qualification.
Absolute or omnibus performance claims without conditions or substantiation.
Superiority or beyond-standard comparative claims requiring benchmark disclosure.

checkpoint_actions: Verify substantiation file exists for superlative claims
Check whether testimonial is attributed and verifiable
Qualify omnibus wording with typical-results framing
escalation_hints: Escalate when absolute cure or miracle language appears on health products
Escalate when comparative superiority lacks named benchmark
